revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:千勝神社 (久喜市吉羽)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chikatsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kuki Yoshiba Chikatsu Shrine Haiden 1.JPG |location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|qid=Q47535|lt=Kuki|1=Kuki, Saitama}} Yoshiba 1-chome |coordinates = {{Coord|36.06611111111111|N|139.68702777777779|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |country_code = JP |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |Rank = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |established = |main_building = |alternate_name = Sansha-sama (三社様) |festival = First Rooster Day in July<ref>『久喜市史調査報告書 第3集 久喜の祭りと行事（87ページ）』 久喜市史編さん室 編集 久喜市 発行 昭和59th year 3月25日 発行</ref> |rituals = |location = {{ill|Kuki|qid=Q47535|1=Kuki, Saitama}} }} '''Chikatsu Shrine''' (千勝神社, Chikatsu-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yoshiba 1-chome, {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|qid=Q47535|lt=Kuki|1=Kuki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. == Overview == Chikatsu Shrine was once a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in Yoshiba Village, Saitama District (later Yoshiba Oaza after the Meiji mergers). Before the Yoshiba Land Readjustment Project, the shrine's location was Oaza Yoshiba, Aza Nishi 1545. Following the October 26, 2002, name change, it became Yoshiba 1-chome 311. Prior to the land readjustment, the precinct area measured 1,042 tsubo (approximately 3,444 m²) in 1948.<ref>『久喜市史調査報告書 第一集 地誌（188ページ）』 久喜市史編さん室 編集 久喜市 発行 昭和58th year 3月25日 発行</ref> The shrine was originally located further south, with a path over 100 meters long, but road widening and development around 1970 led to its relocation northward.<ref>[https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/997741/www.city.kuki.lg.jp/info/koubunsyo/tenji/no20/p11.pdf 第20回企画展 図録『写真と地図で見る太田の変貌 - 特に戦後の吉羽地区を中心として - （9ページ）』 久喜市公文書館 旧ホームページ（PDFFile）] - 国立国会図書館（インターネット資料収集保存事業）</ref> Within the precinct are subordinate shrines, including {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (stone monument), {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} (shaden), and {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q514480}} (shaden). As of 1870, Chikatsu Shrine had absorbed {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} (dedicated to Takemikazuchi and Futsunushi) and {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|qid=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}} (dedicated to Ame-no-Mikuma-no-Kami), and was thus also called "Sansha Daimyōjin" (三社大明神). Precinct features include the {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|qid=Q2044994|lt=main shrine (honden)|1=Honden}}, a {{ill|shamusho|ja|社務所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shamusho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q845945}}, {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}}, {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=Chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1069667}}, commemorative stone monuments, stone lanterns, an 1829 (Bunsei 12) pair of stone komainu, large coniferous trees, and a restroom. The festival is held on the first Rooster Day in July, a tradition linked to the incorporation of Washinomiya Shrine. According to legend, the deity of Washinomiya Shrine sought refuge at Chikatsu Shrine while fleeing bandits. This occurred on a Rooster Day, thus establishing the date for the festival. Festival customs included families preparing sekihan (red rice) in the morning, distributing it to relatives, making wheat buns at noon, and udon noodles in the evening. Lanterns were placed along pathways near the shrine by assigned participants.<ref>『久喜市史調査報告書 第3集 久喜の祭りと行事（39ページ）』 久喜市史編さん室 編集 久喜市 発行 昭和59th year 3月25日 発行</ref> On the day after the festival, a ritual known as "Naida" took place in the Oaza Yoshiba area. This exorcism ritual involved a procession carrying a large rosary, led by a gong from {{ill|Takanawaji Temple|ja|高輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanawaji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while chanting "Naida, Naida." The procession, originally visiting each household, later focused on key crossroads, ending at Nakaochihori River.<ref>『久喜市史調査報告書 第3集 久喜の祭りと行事（40ページ）』 久喜市史編さん室 編集 久喜市 発行 昭和59th year 3月25日 発行</ref> == Yasaka Shrine == Within Chikatsu Shrine's precinct is the subordinate '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社, Yasaka-jinja), dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. It features a main hall, but its origins are unknown.<ref name="#1"/> == Inari Shrine == Another subordinate shrine within the precinct is '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷社, Inari-sha). Previously located at Oaza Yoshiba, Aza Nishi 1562, it was moved to Chikatsu Shrine's northwest corner in 1984. Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, the former precinct measured 75 tsubo (approximately 248 m²) in 1948.<ref>『平成19th year 度 久喜市市民大学 大学院研究論文集Ⅰ 久喜市神社と神域の護持像、狛犬の調査（79ページ）』 久喜市市民大学 発行 平成20th year 2月16日 発行</ref><ref>『久喜市史調査報告書 第一集 地誌（189ページ）』 久喜市史編さん室 編集 久喜市 発行 昭和58th year 3月25日 発行</ref> == Other Chikatsu Shrines in Kuki City == * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Honcho)|ja|千勝神社 (久喜市本町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Motomachi)|qq=}} - Located in Motomachi 6-chome. * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Kamihayami)|ja|千勝社 (久喜市上早見)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Kamihayami)|qq=}} - Located in Kamihayami, Shinden. * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Nakazuma)|ja|千勝神社 (久喜市中妻)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Nakazuma)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505735|1=Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Nakazuma)}} - Located in Nakazuma. * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Isaka)|ja|千勝神社 (久喜市伊坂)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Isaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505739|1=Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Isaka)}} - Located in Isaka. == Related Sites and Nearby locations == * {{ill|Yoshiba Park|ja|吉羽公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiba Park|1=Yoshiba Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11414215}} * {{ill|Kuki City Ota Elementary School|ja|久喜市立太田小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuki City Ota Elementary School|1=Kuki City Ota Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369226}} * {{ill|Kuki City Library (Central Library)|ja|久喜市立図書館#中央図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuki City Library (Central Library)|1=Kuki City Library (Central Library)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369225}} * {{ill|Takanawaji Temple|ja|高輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanawaji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * <del>{{ill|Inaridai Canal|ja|稲荷台用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai Canal|1=Inaridai Canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11596851}}</del> - A former waterway flowing northwest to southeast near the shrine. == Access == * From {{ill|Kuki Station (Saitama)|en|Kuki Station (Saitama)|qid=Q843719|lt=Kuki Station|1=Kuki Station (Saitama)}} east exit, take the {{ill|Asahi Bus|ja|朝日バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朝日自動車|1=Asahi Motor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11517584}} (Stop 2) for "Yoshiba/Kurihara Circular" or "Kurihara via Asahi Bus Depot." Alight at "Yoshiba Park" (a11rox. 5 minutes), then walk south for 2 minutes (a11rox. 100 m). Fare: 100 yen.<ref>[http://yahoo.jp/HtnlaB Chikatsu Shrine Surroundings] - Yahoo! Loco Homepage</ref> * From Kuki Station east exit, take the {{ill|Kuki City Circulation Bus|ja|久喜市内循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuki City Circulation Bus|1=Kuki City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133711061}}, "Nokuki-Yoshiba Circular." Alight at "Ota Elementary School" (a11rox. 17 minutes) or "City Library" (a11rox. 18 minutes). From "Ota Elementary School," walk south-southwest (a11rox. 150 m). From "City Library," walk west (a11rox. 200 m). Fare: 100 yen (one-day pass: 200 yen). [https://www.city.kuki.lg.jp/kurashi/norimono_anzen/shinaijyunkan_bus/junkanbus.html (City Circulation Bus - Kuki City Homepage)] == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == External Links == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kuki.lg.jp/miryoku/kanko_tokusan/map2.files/map01.pdf "Kuki Station East Exit Course" Kuki City Sightseeing Walking Map]|qq=}} - Kuki City Homepage * [http://kuki-sci.sakura.ne.jp/midokoro/pg118.html Kuki Walking Map - Interaction Course] - {{ill|Kuki City Chamber of Commerce|ja|久喜市商工会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuki City Chamber of Commerce|1=Kuki City Chamber of Commerce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17214116}} Homepage * [https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/997741/www.city.kuki.lg.jp/info/koubunsyo/tenji/no20/index.html 第20回企画展 図録『写真と地図で見る太田の変貌 - 特に戦後の吉羽地区を中心として - （9・12・13・14ページ）』 久喜市公文書館 旧ホームページ] - {{ill|National Diet Library|en|National Diet Library|qid=Q477675|lt=National Diet Library|1=National Diet Library}} Internet Resources Preservation Project * [http://glassesmaiden.blog81.fc2.com/blog-entry-1232.html "Chikatsu Shrine (Yoshiba 1-chome)"] - Shrine Guda Guda Visit Records Homepage Hasshinden
{{translated page|ja|千勝神社 (久喜市吉羽)|version=102266255|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:千勝神社 (久喜市吉羽)]]
